title: 'I didn't think I'd be graduating' after Freshers' Week cancer op

input.lead: Ella Tyman earns a first-class honours degree following cancer treatment after arriving at university
input.summary: Ella Tyman earns a first-class honours degree following cancer treatment after arriving at university
input.evidence_text: Ella Tyman had just moved into university accommodation in the autumn of 2023, and was about to enjoy Freshers' Week, when she was told she was needed urgent surgery for cancer The 21-year-old, of Skelmersdale, Lancashire, had been diagnosed with parotid gland cancer after doctors found a lump behind her ear during an appointment for an eye infection Ella said she had believed the cancer could be quickly removed before returning to life at the University of Salford. But a planned 45-minute procedure became a six-hour operation after the cancer had doubled in size and wrapped around facial nerves "I didn't think I'd be graduating now," she said, having achieved a first-class honours degree "They had to cut the nerves in my face," she said "I didn't even want to walk out of my house, let alone go to university."

title: Burnham's 'Manchesterism' got him to No 10 - but will it work for the UK?

input.lead: Economics editor Faisal Islam examines whether the north-west city can be a template for the entire country
input.summary: Economics editor Faisal Islam examines whether the north-west city can be a template for the entire country
input.evidence_text: By Faisal Islam Economics editor It was just five months ago when Andy Burnham retreated to his mayor's office in Manchester, having been blocked by Labour's ruling executive from standing for parliament When I met him there a few weeks later, he told me he planned to deal with his disappointment with some ambitious plans for his city region Burnham told me he wanted to appeal directly to Fifa to host the final of the women's football World Cup in 2035 Manchester instead of Wembley. "Imagine how electrifying that is for any girl growing up in the north of England," he said He said he was also joining forces with other mayors for a "Great Northern" Olympic bid across the north of England, and a plan was also afoot to host the Ryder Cup in Bolton. Sports bodies needed "re-educating" about the rest of the country, he said Manchester has already poached the Brit Awards from London after half a century in the capital Big, bold gestures like these tell part of the story of what has happened in the city. title: Man found guilty of murdering boy who stole e-bike in Middleton

input.lead: Terrence King, 60, is convicted of killing 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the bike
input.summary: Terrence King, 60, is convicted of killing 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the bike
input.evidence_text: Man found guilty of murdering boy who stole e-bike in Middleton - BBC News Image source, GMP Image caption, Terrence King was convicted following a two-week trial By Phil Cunliffe North West Published 17 July 2026 A man has been found guilty of murdering a teenager who stole an e-bike by deliberately driving his car into him. Terrence King, 60, killed 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who had stolen the £3,000 bike, from another boy in Middleton in Greater Manchester on 22 July 2025. The jury was told King had admitted manslaughter and accepted his actions caused Devon's death, but he denied murder. But he was found guilty of murder following a two-week trial and will be sentenced on 23 July at Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester. There were screams of joy from the victim's family and friends inside the courtroom as the guilty verdict was announced along with shouts of "thank you". CCTV and doorbell camera footage showed Simmonds-Caines riding the e-bike, which the court heard he - along with a friend - had stolen from another boy on the Langley Estate. There was a fracas and punches thrown before Devon rode off on the e-bike, the court heard. Image source, GMP Image caption, Devon Simmonds-Caines, 15, died in Middleton in July 2025 Outlining the case, prosecuting barrister Michael Hayton KC said King wanted to cause Devon "at the very least, really serious harm" and had steered his car onto the pavement without applying the brakes. "It was not a tragic motor accident or poor driving" he told the jury. He said it was the Crown's case that King went "from being outraged" to being someone who made "a criminal decision". "This is not an attempt to recover the bike but to get revenge or punish the boy," he said. King's car was later seen driving at speed on the road

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Greater Manchester Police gets top grade in new quality rating

input.lead: The new ranking comes four years after the force was taken out of special measures
input.summary: The new ranking comes four years after the force was taken out of special measures
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police gets top grade in new quality rating - BBC News Image source, PA Media Image caption, Greater Manchester Police was placed in special measures in 2020 By Dan Wareing North West Published 17 July 2026 Greater Manchester Police has been given the highest possible rating in a new national quality ranking system, four years after it was taken out of special measures. The force has been awarded Level 1 status in the Home Office's Police Performance System, which was introduced by the government in April. The rating means it requires no action or intervention. GMP said the outcome was "a source of considerable encouragement and one in which every one of its members can take legitimate pride". Chief Constable Sir Stephen Watson said: "Whilst this is an occasion upon which we should reflect with some satisfaction, we should also recognise that performance is not a destination but a continuing endeavour. "The communities we serve rightly expect us to strive constantly for improvement and we remain ambitious and restless in that pursuit." In 2020, the force was put into special measures after a report found it had failed to record 80,000 crimes in a year. The matter led to the resignation of the former chief constable. Two years later it was taken out of the measures, and then the following year the watchdog found the force had significantly improved . title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: Two more arson arrests over moorland fire at Dove Stone Reservoir

input.lead: Three people have now been arrested in connection with the fire, which broke out on Saturday
input.summary: Three people have now been arrested in connection with the fire, which broke out on Saturday
input.evidence_text: Two more arson arrests over moorland fire at Dove Stone Reservoir - BBC News Image source, Reuters Image caption, The fire broke out near Dove Stone Reservoir in Greenfield, Saddleworth, on Saturday By Dan Wareing North West Published 17 July 2026 Two more people have been arrested on suspicion of arson after a fire broke out on a Greater Manchester moorland. The blaze started last Saturday at Dove Stone Reservoir in Greenfield, Saddleworth, and is still burning, with smoke being carried across the region. Great Manchester Police said a man in his 20s was arrested earlier on suspicion of arson, while a woman was arrested on Thursday on the same suspicion. The woman has been released on bail. The arrests follow the charging of Shania Care-Slede, 20, of Market Street, Hyde, with aggravated arson, being reckless as to whether life was endangered, and dangerous driving. She is due to appear at Manchester Crown Court on 18 August. 'Fire still ongoing' "This investigation has been progressing at pace. We are nearly a week on and the fire at Dove Stone is still ongoing, and it has had devastating effects on the reservoir," said Det Insp Andrew Day. He reminded people not to share posts on social media about the incident while the police investigation was ongoing. "As this incident continues, officers would like to thank all emergency service partners for their support and dedication. "In particular, our colleagues at Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service, whose firefighters have spent countless hours tackling the fire in demanding conditions," he said. Road closures remain in place on Holmfirth Road and Bank Lane. There is no access to Dove Stone Reservoir, and members of the public are being told to avoid the area. title: Prisoner arrested after escaping during hospital transfer

input.lead: Marwan Jumaa, who is serving a sentence for attacking two women, escaped on Tuesday evening
input.summary: Marwan Jumaa, who is serving a sentence for attacking two women, escaped on Tuesday evening
input.evidence_text: Prisoner arrested after escaping during hospital transfer - BBC News Image source, GMP Image caption, Marwan Jumaa is classed as a risk to others and himself By Paul Burnell , North West and Harry Sekulich Published 16 July 2026 Updated 17 July 2026 Police have arrested a prisoner who escaped during a transfer from a secure hospital facility to North Manchester Hospital on Tuesday evening. Marwan Jumaa, 20, is in Lancashire Constabulary custody following a manhunt, Greater Manchester Police said in the early hours of Friday. "An observant Lancashire Constabulary officer recognised Jumaa and acted swiftly to arrest him," Detective Supt Nicola McCulloch said in a statement. "He will now be returned to a secure hospital facility." Officers had issued an appeal for information after Jumaa, who is serving an indefinite sentence for attacking two women, absconded during the hospital transfer. He was described as a risk to others and himself. The public had been warned to not approach him, but were instead urged to contact authorities. McCulloch continued: "This has been a fast-paced investigation which has involved a significant number of officers and resources from Greater Manchester Police. "I would like to thank the public for their support following our appeal. Our call handlers received a considerable amount of information from members of the public and that assistance has been invaluable in helping us locate Jumaa. "We are extremely grateful for the support shown throughout this investigation." input.summary: A moorland blaze which led to the closure of a major road linking South Yorkshire and Greater Manchester was started by a crashed car bursting into flames Firefighters were called to the A628 Woodhead Pass near Flouch at about 13:30 BST on Thursday to reports a car had come off t
input.evidence_text: A moorland blaze which led to the closure of a major road linking South Yorkshire and Greater Manchester was started by a crashed car bursting into flames Firefighters were called to the A628 Woodhead Pass near Flouch at about 13:30 BST on Thursday to reports a car had come off the road and set on fire Fire station manager Jonathan Poynter said the flames moved quickly due to the dry ground, and required "copious amounts of water" to put them out "At its peak, it wasn't so much about the flame height and the head of the fire, but due to the nature of the land and the peatiness, it can get deep really quick, so that was the main concern for the crews," he said Poynter said crews left the scene at about 23:00 BST, with periodic checks carried out throughout the evening. No injuries were reported Firefighters returned on Friday morning to continue damping down, while a drone was used to carry out thermal imaging of the site

title: The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way

input.lead: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.summary: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felled by a Taser, the balaclava-clad figure rolled onto his back and raised a 9mm pistol Both hands wrapped around the grip he aimed the loaded gun straight at the police officer stood just a few feet away. "I could see the barrel pointing right at me and he was following my movements as I was trying to get away from him," Pc Dan Clayton would later recall "In that moment I was thinking of my pregnant wife and how she might have to bring up our child alone." Despite having his life flash before his eyes, the brave GMP cop remained remarkably calm. 'Gun, gun, gun' he shouted as he warned off a council parking warden who was trying to intervene And as he took shelter behind a parked car he also memorised a comprehensive description of the gunman, which just 24 hours later, would help lead to his capture. Pc Clayton's actions have now seen him presented with the Salford Bravery Award

input.evidence_text: 15:29, 17 Jul 2026 Hello, Andy Burnham was confirmed as the new leader of the Labour Party this afternoon. And as he gave his first speech after succeeding Sir Keir Starmer he vowed to 'put hope in every heart' Speaking at a special conference at the Trades Union Congress headquarters in central London before he becomes Prime Minister on Monday the Makerfield MP and former Greater Manchester Mayor promised to 'get the country pulling together again' and vowed to change the Labour Party He said: "I want people to say once again that Labour are ‘for us’. We can be that party, the party that puts more power in people’s hands, drives good growth in every postcode and puts hope in every heart.” He also repeated his pledge to devolve power away from Westminster, giving people 'more power over life’s essentials'. He said: "We want to give your area more power to build the council and social homes that you desperately need for those families… more power to improve your hig And he sought to counter criticism of him being too focused on Manchester "Yes, the north of England has given me so much, everything, in fact,” he said. “And in return, I have sought to give it the strongest voice I could. That was my job, but now I do the same for everywhere, because I see the same challenges everywhere I look."

title: 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency'

input.lead: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second.
input.summary: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging
input.evidence_text: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging over Manchester city centre and surrounding towns caused by the moorland will clear. But for the wildlife living on the scorched earth near Dovestone Reservoir in Oldham and on Tintwistle Moor near Glossop the impact has been devastating The moors are home to a rich variety of bird species. Meadow pipits flit, blackcaps move shyly and buzzards soar. The sound of the skylark's life-enhancing rhapsody rings out on both moors and the rare rock-loving Ring Ouzel known as the "mountain blackbird" breeds in the Peak Di But experts say the fires have caused "mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency". Scarred by walls of flames it will take years for the land to recover One woman has been remanded in custody after being charged with arson in connection with the moorland fire near Dovestone reservoir. Shania Care-Slede, 20, appeared at Manchester Magistrates' Court on July 14 after being charged with two offences View 11 Images Moorland fires raged for days above Dovestone Reservoir , Saddleworth in Oldham . Helicopters are used water from the reservoir to dampen the fire . 12 July 2026 (Image: Sean Hansford | Manchester Evening News)

title: 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion

input.lead: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost.
input.summary: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. The local The idea to build a 14-mile t
input.evidence_text: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. The local The idea to build a 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could end the ‘frustration of traffic congestion’ plaguing one corner of Greater Manchester The Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead. The scheme, named ‘Trans-Pennine Connect’, could cut travel times between the two cities by 30 minutes and The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 Reducing congestion around the Mottram area of Tameside has been a long-held ambition of the local MP Jonathan Reynolds. He has successfully campaigned for the A57 link road scheme that is currently under construction This tunnel plan, if it ever gets the go ahead, would connect to the link road scheme that is due to be completed around 2030. Mr Reynolds has welcomed the idea that could benefit motorists coming through his patch

title: The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way

input.lead: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.summary: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felled by a Taser, the balaclava-clad figure rolled onto his back and raised a 9mm pistol Both hands wrapped around the grip he aimed the loaded gun straight at the police officer stood just a few feet away. "I could see the barrel pointing right at me and he was following my movements as I was trying to get away from him," Pc Dan Clayton would later recall "In that moment I was thinking of my pregnant wife and how she might have to bring up our child alone." Despite having his life flash before his eyes, the brave GMP cop remained remarkably calm. 'Gun, gun, gun' he shouted as he warned off a council parking warden who was trying to intervene And as he took shelter behind a parked car he also memorised a comprehensive description of the gunman, which just 24 hours later, would help lead to his capture. Pc Clayton's actions have now seen him presented with the Salford Bravery Award

Lee Williams, 19, (11/08/2006); John Harte, 19, (08/01/2007); and Riley McDermott, 18, (16/03/2008); all denied murder. Maughan was later seen on CCTV speaking to doorman Zhahed Mahmood, 42. As officers arrived at the Town Hall Square venue, Mahmood was caught on body cam footage telling Maughan: "Get off, get out." Maughan was jailed for a year and three months having pleaded guilty to wounding. title: 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency'

input.summary: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging
input.evidence_text: Fires 'It's a mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency' "Weeks of hard work raising chicks will have been gone in a second. Whole families wiped out." News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:46, 18 Jul 2026 Updated 07:47, 18 Jul 2026 View 11 Imag Eventually the smog hanging over Manchester city centre and surrounding towns caused by the moorland will clear. But for the wildlife living on the scorched earth near Dovestone Reservoir in Oldham and on Tintwistle Moor near Glossop the impact has been devastating The moors are home to a rich variety of bird species. Meadow pipits flit, blackcaps move shyly and buzzards soar. The sound of the skylark's life-enhancing rhapsody rings out on both moors and the rare rock-loving Ring Ouzel known as the "mountain blackbird" breeds in the Peak Di But experts say the fires have caused "mass fatality and a state of ecological emergency". Scarred by walls of flames it will take years for the land to recover One woman has been remanded in custody after being charged with arson in connection with the moorland fire near Dovestone reservoir. Shania Care-Slede, 20, appeared at Manchester Magistrates' Court on July 14 after being charged with two offences View 11 Images Moorland fires raged for days above Dovestone Reservoir , Saddleworth in Oldham . Helicopters are used water from the reservoir to dampen the fire . 12 July 2026 (Image: Sean Hansford | Manchester Evening News)

input.summary: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. The local The idea to build a 14-mile t
input.evidence_text: Transport 14-mile underground dual carriageway between Manchester and Sheffield tipped to end traffic congestion Original plans to create the dual carriageway under the Peak District between Manchester and Sheffield was shelved due to cost. The local The idea to build a 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could end the ‘frustration of traffic congestion’ plaguing one corner of Greater Manchester The Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead. The scheme, named ‘Trans-Pennine Connect’, could cut travel times between the two cities by 30 minutes and The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 Reducing congestion around the Mottram area of Tameside has been a long-held ambition of the local MP Jonathan Reynolds. He has successfully campaigned for the A57 link road scheme that is currently under construction This tunnel plan, if it ever gets the go ahead, would connect to the link road scheme that is due to be completed around 2030. Mr Reynolds has welcomed the idea that could benefit motorists coming through his patch

title: The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way

input.lead: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.summary: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felle
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police The masked drug dealer with a loaded gun and the cop who stood in his way The 'epitome of bravery' View 5 Images Jay Conway pointing a loaded gun at Pc Dan Clayton (Image: GMP) News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:30, 18 Jul 2026 As he lay in the road, felled by a Taser, the balaclava-clad figure rolled onto his back and raised a 9mm pistol Both hands wrapped around the grip he aimed the loaded gun straight at the police officer stood just a few feet away. "I could see the barrel pointing right at me and he was following my movements as I was trying to get away from him," Pc Dan Clayton would later recall "In that moment I was thinking of my pregnant wife and how she might have to bring up our child alone." Despite having his life flash before his eyes, the brave GMP cop remained remarkably calm. 'Gun, gun, gun' he shouted as he warned off a council parking warden who was trying to intervene And as he took shelter behind a parked car he also memorised a comprehensive description of the gunman, which just 24 hours later, would help lead to his capture. Pc Clayton's actions have now seen him presented with the Salford Bravery Award

He said: "We want to give your area more power to build the council and social homes that you desperately need for those families… more power to improve your hig And he sought to counter criticism of him being too focused on Manchester "Yes, the north of England has given me so much, everything, in fact,” he said. “And in return, I have sought to give it the strongest voice I could. That was my job, but now I do the same for everywhere, because I see the same challenges everywhere I look."

Andy Burnham has vowed to 'put hope in every heart' as he gave his first speech after being confirmed as the new leader of the Labour Party . Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

To learn more about the development, search for reference number DC/096927 on Stockport Council’s planning portal

input.evidence_text: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a The new pizzeria, located at 237-239 Monton Road , will transform the former Bennetts Furnishers store that has been vacant since 2022, with work now officially underway. The spacious venue will mark Rudy’s 41st restaurant to date Since launching in Manchester’s Ancoats in 2015, Rudy’s has grown into one of the UK’s most loved pizzerias, celebrated for its light, double fermented dough and unwavering dedication to true Neopolitan craft Testament to its authenticity, Rudy’s is the largest organisation in the world to be accredited by the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN), the international body that protects and promotes traditional Neapolitan pizza making Rudy’s has also been recognised among the World’s 50 Best Pizzeria, ranking 23rd globally for 2025. Each pizza, from the classic Margherita to the fiery Calabrese, is made to AVPN standards using traditional techniques and ingredients sourced directly from Italy To claim one of 3,000 complimentary pizzas, Monton locals can sign up via this link , where they will be able to choose from Rudy’s favourites such as its Cinghiale , Margherita Con Bufala, and Triple Pepperoni to enjoy at the new opening this summer

title: GetFaked: how a star baker confected his enemies

input.lead: Rich Myers went viral for airing his haters online.
input.summary: Rich Myers went viral for airing his haters online. Turns out they weren’t real
input.evidence_text: Rich Myers went viral for airing his haters online. Turns out they weren’t real Dear readers — today’s edition comes to you in partnership with our brand-new sister paper, The Exchange! Our colleagues over in Leeds have been hurting their carpal tunnels over the mixing bowl, cooking up a piece about Manchester and Leed’s most infamous baker: Rich Myers of Ge “ I’m an insecure guy to be completely honest,” the 37-year-old chef says. I’m surprised at the turn the conversation has taken. When I contacted Myers for comment on a piece (this piece in fact) I’m writing about GetBaked – his unprecedentedly successful yet controversial bakery But an hour later, we’re still on the phone and the conversation is getting deep “Sometimes I massively regret being the face,” says Myers of his business. This is unexpected: GetBaked might just be Leeds’ biggest export since Tom Zanetti. title: A dish best served cold: A TV historian sued us to reveal our sources - and lost

input.evidence_text: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. Claire, representing Mill Media, asked him about the dish “best served cold”

title: Why severe wildfires are becoming more frequent in the UK and how to cope with the health risks

input.lead: Wildfires are more common in the UK than many people realise, although most are typically small and last a short time.
input.summary: Wildfires are more common in the UK than many people realise, although most are typically small and last a short time. Hot, dry weather can dry out vegetation providing fuel for fires and increase the risk of wildfires. Climate
input.evidence_text: Wildfires are more common in the UK than many people realise, although most are typically small and last a short time. Hot, dry weather can dry out vegetation providing fuel for fires and increase the risk of wildfires. Climate change projections suggest we will see conditions mo In 2025, fire and rescue services in England and Wales responded to nearly 1,000 wildfire incidents - surpassing the previous record set in 2022. This year, widespread wildfires have led to headlines about a ‘firewave’. With hotter, drier summers becoming more common, it’s worth A wildfire is any uncontrolled vegetation fire serious enough to require an emergency response. Typically, they include fires covering at least a hectare of land (approximately the size of 2 rugby pitches), with flames over 1.5 metres high, or incidents requiring fire crews for 6 Increasingly, yes. Climate change is making our summers hotter and drier - ideal conditions for fire. When landscapes experience several consecutive days of dry weather and little or no rain, plants and soils lose moisture faster than they can recover. Vegetation becomes a more a It takes very little vegetation (fuel) for a wildfire to start and it is important to follow wildfire prevention advice issued by the Fire and Rescue Service . Most wildfires start due to human activities and are a risk to communities, emergency services as well as increasing pre All smoke is potentially harmful to your health. title: Oldham bucks national trend as more young people choose education and training

input.lead: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.summary: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.evidence_text: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET) The latest Department for Education (DfE) Local Authority Participation and NEET figures for the 2025/26 academic year show that 93.2 per cent of Oldham's 16 and 17-year-olds are participating in education and training, an increase of 2.8 percentage points on the previous year. T At the same time, Oldham's combined NEET and Not Known (NK) rate has fallen to 4.6%, down 1.1 percentage points from 2024/25. This is significantly lower than both the North West average of 5.5% and the England average of 5.8% The figures also highlight Oldham's strong performance, in partnership with the charity Positive Steps, in tracking and supporting young people Positive Steps provides young people with support, careers advice and guidance after school, or between 19 and 25 if they have an Education Healthcare Plan (EHCP), helping them to move into education, training or employment Only 59 young people out of 7,177 are currently unaccounted for – 0.8 per cent of the borough's young people. This is well below the North West average of 1.3 per cent and the England average of 2.4 per cent

input.evidence_text: Oldham Council and local charity Northern Roots are celebrating a major milestone in the creation of Northern Roots – set to become the UK’s largest urban farm and eco-park The council has officially taken handover of its new Welcome Building and Forestry Skills Centre from contractors Willmott Dixon, marking a significant step towards the opening of the pioneering attraction on Saturday 19 September Delivered through a partnership between the Northern Roots charity and Oldham Council, the project is transforming 160-acres of underused land next to Alexandra Park into a landmark destination for nature, health, learning, enterprise and community activity Five years in the making, Northern Roots is one of the most ambitious projects of its kind in the UK, using the power of nature to improve health and wellbeing, create jobs and training opportunities and bring communities together At the heart of the site is the new Welcome Centre, which features a café, shop and production kitchen, creating a vibrant hub for visitors. The building also includes two flexible spaces for school visits, community events, meetings, exhibitions and performances When the site opens, visitors will be able to enjoy an expanding network of accessible green spaces, orchards, community gardens, food-growing areas and nature-rich landscapes. input.evidence_text: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale This follows a careers event organised by Rochdale’s additional needs service (RANS), part of Rochdale Borough Council, at Middleton Arena to inspire the career aspirations of local pupils aged 10-14 with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) During a day packed full of careers advice, more than 60 pupils from nearby schools had the opportunity to hear from a number of successful professionals with additional needs This included 2-time Paralympic gold medallist and double amputee runner, Richard Whitehead MBE, who also holds the world record for the fastest marathon by a leg amputee This event came as national figures highlight only half the number of people in the UK with a disability aged 16-64 are in employment (52.8%), compared to 4 out of 5 people (82.5%) who don’t have a disability Local services have been working hard however, to address this challenge by highlighting the successful careers and futures that young people can achieve with a disability as they grow up

input.lead: Press release — 16 July 2026 19:18 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied!
input.summary: Press release — 16 July 2026 19:18 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download People looking for affordable, flexible housing and residents living in Bury's neighbourhoods will benefit from new guidance adopted by Bury Council to support sustainable, inclusive growth
input.evidence_text: Press release — 16 July 2026 19:18 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download People looking for affordable, flexible housing and residents living in Bury's neighbourhoods will benefit from new guidance adopted by Bury Council to support sustainable, inclusive growth across the borough The new Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) Supplementary Planning Document (SPD) recognises the important role HMOs play in providing accommodation for young professionals, key workers, people on lower incomes and those with short-term housing needs The guidance will help ensure future HMOs are well-designed and well-managed, supporting housing choice while protecting the character, wellbeing and balance of local communities The new SPD introduces clear planning criteria to help prevent excessive concentrations of HMOs. These include a 10% concentration threshold within a 100-metre radius, measures to prevent single-family homes from being “sandwiched” between HMOs, and restrictions on creating three At the same time, the SPD seeks to address concerns associated with poorly located or over-concentrated HMOs, such as parking pressures, noise, waste management issues and the loss of balanced communities

title: Helping Bury families make healthier food choices

input.lead: Children and families across Bury will benefit from healthier neighbourhoods after Bury Council adopted new planning guidance to help improve local food environments and support healthier lifestyles

input.evidence_text: Press release — 15 July 2026 11:05 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Children and families across Bury will benefit from healthier neighbourhoods after Bury Council adopted new planning guidance to help improve local food environments and support healthier lifestyles The new guidance will help shape decisions on future hot food takeaway developments, supporting efforts to reduce health inequalities and give residents, particularly young people, better opportunities to eat well and live well Bury Council has formally adopted its Hot Food Takeaway Supplementary Planning Document (SPD), strengthening the borough's commitment to creating healthier communities and reducing health inequalities Developed through close collaboration between Public Health and Planning, the SPD uses local data and national evidence to guide decisions on new hot food takeaway developments. Research shows that higher concentrations of takeaways are often linked to increased childhood obesity A key feature of the guidance is Bury's Hot Food Takeaway Matrix, which helps inform planning decisions by considering factors such as proximity to secondary schools, local childhood obesity rates and existing concentrations of takeaway outlets

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

input.evidence_text: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat? title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.

input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. With a capacity of over 53,000, state-of-the-art facilities, and a great atmosphere on matchdays, the Etihad Stadium is not only the club’s home but also a key part of Manchester’s sporting and cultural id

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. With a capacity of over 53,000, state-of-the-art facilities, and a great atmosphere on matchdays, the Etihad Stadium is not only the club’s home but also a key part of Manchester’s sporting and cultural id

title: Young Musician's Summer School

input.lead: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer!
input.summary: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! […] The post Young Musician’s Summer School appeared first on Band on the Wall
input.evidence_text: Young Musician’s Summer School . Young Musician’s Summer School. . Book here . . . . . . . Friday | 03.07.2026 . . . World of Music . Share . . . . --> . . We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. In this show Mike talks about doing psychedelics with his brother, an entanglement with a Spanish Sausage Dog named Nino, and many other such rotten adventures ‘ Ireland has yet another legendary comedian ‘ – Dan Nightingale Irish farmer’s son, and general Nasty Character Mike Rice is one of Ireland and the UK’s fastest rising comedy stars. Having started comedy in 2016 Mike has amassed a large online following from podcast and stand-up clips, regularly getting millions of views on various platforms Mike began stand-up comedy in Chicago, where within two years he was working regularly at the most prestigious clubs in the city, including The Laugh Factory. He has since returned home because his mother made him, and has been quickly rising in the Irish and European scene, play Mike brought his first solo show The Best Boy in Ireland to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2018, in what was a sold-out run, and performed that same show to sold-out rooms at the Perth Fringe Festival in 2019 As well as co-hosting Mike & Vittorio’s Guide to Parenting with Vittorio Angelone, and Big Mike and The Chief with Rob Moriarty, Mike has appeared multiple times on smash hit podcast Have a Word and released his first comedy special in October 2023

title: Rudy's to open in Monton Salford - About Manchester

input.summary: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a
input.evidence_text: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a The new pizzeria, located at 237-239 Monton Road , will transform the former Bennetts Furnishers store that has been vacant since 2022, with work now officially underway. The spacious venue will mark Rudy’s 41st restaurant to date Since launching in Manchester’s Ancoats in 2015, Rudy’s has grown into one of the UK’s most loved pizzerias, celebrated for its light, double fermented dough and unwavering dedication to true Neopolitan craft Testament to its authenticity, Rudy’s is the largest organisation in the world to be accredited by the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN), the international body that protects and promotes traditional Neapolitan pizza making Rudy’s has also been recognised among the World’s 50 Best Pizzeria, ranking 23rd globally for 2025. Each pizza, from the classic Margherita to the fiery Calabrese, is made to AVPN standards using traditional techniques and ingredients sourced directly from Italy To claim one of 3,000 complimentary pizzas, Monton locals can sign up via this link , where they will be able to choose from Rudy’s favourites such as its Cinghiale , Margherita Con Bufala, and Triple Pepperoni to enjoy at the new opening this summer

input.summary: Wednesday, 22nd July at 15:00 BST | 16:00 CET / SAST AI adoption is accelerating across every industry, but innovation without governance introduces new risks. From sensitive data being shared with public AI tools to evolving regulatory requirements, organisations must ensure AI
input.evidence_text: Governing AI: Building trust, managing risk, and staying compliant | Manchester Digital Governing AI: Building trust, managing risk, and staying compliant View Profile View More Events 22 July 2026 15:00 - 15:45 Book Now Wednesday, 22nd July at 15:00 BST | 16:00 CET / SAST 08:00 MTD | 10:00 EST | 11:00 AST Speakers Brian Martin , Director of Product Management, Integrity360 Seán Brennan , CRA Practice Manager, Integrity360 AI adoption is accelerating across every industry, but innovation without governance introduces new risks. From sensitive data being shared with public AI tools to evolving regulatory requirements, organisations must ensure AI is being deployed responsibly, securely and with appropriate oversight. In this webinar, Brian Martin is joined by Seán Brennan, CRA Practice Manager, Integrity360, to explore how organisations can confidently adopt AI while managing governance, risk and compliance. Drawing on emerging frameworks including ISO/IEC 42001, the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, and the EU AI Act, this session will provide practical guidance on establishing governance for both internally developed AI solutions and the use of public AI platforms. Attendees will gain insight into managing AI-related risks, protecting sensitive information, meeting emerging regulatory expectations, and creating a framework that enables innovation without compromising security or compliance. Join us for a practical discussion on how to build effective AI governance that balances innovation with trust, accountability and risk management. What you will learn: Why AI governance has become a business and regulatory priority The key risks organisations face as AI adoption accelerates How ISO/IEC 42001 provides a framework for governing AI responsibly The role of the NIST AI

title: Prolific North

input.lead: Manchester-based indie Purple Productions, the force behind BBC amateur sleuthing smash Doorbell Detectives, is the first casualty of the newly announced “mega-indie” merger between Banijay and All3Media
input.summary: Manchester-based indie Purple Productions, the force behind BBC amateur sleuthing smash Doorbell Detectives, is the first casualty of the newly announced “mega-indie” merger between Banijay and All3Media

title: Manchester founder moves to Silicon Valley to build AI startup

input.lead: When Erin Meryl McGurk was growing up in South Manchester she could not have imagined where she is today.
input.summary: When Erin Meryl McGurk was growing up in South Manchester she could not have imagined where she is today. The pupil at Altrincham Grammar School for Girls is now building an AI startup in Silicon Valley after securing backing from the prestigious Y Combinator accelerator. “Growin
input.evidence_text: Manchester founder moves to Silicon Valley to build AI startup Posted on July 17, 2026 by Jonathan Symcox Manchester founder moves to Silicon Valley to build AI startup Erin Meryl McGurk met her co-founder while dancing at Cambridge University - now they’re launching a product to end onboarding Share: Founders When Erin Meryl McGurk was growing up in South Manchester she could not have imagined where she is today. The pupil at Altrincham Grammar School for Girls is now building an AI startup in Silicon Valley after securing backing from the prestigious Y Combinator accelerator. “Growing up in South Manchester and going to AGGS, I did not see many people, especially women, taking the route I’m taking now – building a technology company, raising from Y Combinator, and moving to Silicon Valley,” she says. “I’d love to make that path feel more visible and possible for girls who are where I was a few years ago.” McGurk is the creator behind @erinmerylstudy, which has made her one of the UK’s largest and most recognisable educational content creators. Reading Land Economy at St John’s College at the University of Cambridge, she is also now co-founder and CEO of Egoist Machines, which is building AI Passport. This allows people to establish connections to preferred AI assistants and other apps that rely on personal data, including email, calendar, work productivity apps and wearable devices. When a connected app receives or creates new information, such as details for a trip or a new writing preference, that information can appear in the user’s AI Passport inbox. The user can decide whether it can be shared with other apps, kept only in the original app, or deleted entirely. She met her co-founder and CTO Dr David Khachaturov (below) while Caledonian dancing – a form of Scotti

Estimated to be 67 million years old, the original specimen is on display at the Natural History Museum Abu Dhabi The process of moving him to Victoria Baths will be a gargantuan task that will take a team of specialists a full week. Manchester Museum is also working hard behind-the-scenes on a temporary Stan stand-in, with news on that to be ann Georgina Young, Acting Director at Manchester Museum, said: “Manchester Museum cares for around 4.5m objects and taking them to unexpected places creates new opportunities for learning and connection. Ashleigh Beattie covers sections of the gallery with clay dug from her Manchester garden. input.evidence_text: Discover Campfield Market — a modern city market in the heart of Manchester. Every Saturday, explore 70+ independent traders, street food pop-ups, local produce inside the historic Campfield buildings Campfield Market is Manchester’s weekly city market, bringing together street food, local produce and artisanal makers from the city’s best independent traders Inspired by traditional European markets, Campfield Market stretches from under the iconic glass roof out into the streets beyond Now reaching into St John’s and right down Grape Street, it transforms the area into a full kilometre loop of market stalls, street food and independent retail Each week you’ll find a rotating line-up of street food traders alongside European-style food and drink, quality produce and a carefully curated mix of artisan makers With over 100 traders landing every Saturday and a line-up that changes week on week, it’s built for wandering, repeat visits and discovering something new every time A weekly market that supports independents, serves the local community and brings people together every Saturday Campfield Market | Manchester’s New City Market 0 . . . title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-19 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: Roy Keane may be set for last ITV appearance at World Cup as retirement deadline looms

input.lead: Roy Keane has floated the idea of retirement from his Sky Sports punditry job as he shared desires to exit the UK
input.summary: Roy Keane has floated the idea of retirement from his Sky Sports punditry job as he shared desires to exit the UK
input.evidence_text: Football Roy Keane Roy Keane may be set for last ITV appearance at World Cup as retirement deadline looms Roy Keane has floated the idea of retirement from his Sky Sports punditry job as he shared desires to exit the UK Comments Sport Abbie Meehan Senior Sports Writer 06:30 Roy Keane could work his final World Cup match this weekend if he sticks to his retirement plans. The ex- Manchester United midfielder has revealed his desire to step away from punditry and move abroad Keane spent a large part of his career at Old Trafford , working under Sir Alex Ferguson and winning seven Premier League titles. He hung up his boots in 2006 and moved into management, coaching Sunderland and Ipswich Town However, the former club captain saw his career away from the pitch truly take off when he moved into punditry to work for ITV and Sky Sports. Keane is a regular on The Overlap and works closely with Jill Scott , Ian Wright, Jamie Carragher and ex-teammate Gary Neville During an episode recorded in 2024, the 54-year-old admitted he was contemplating leaving the UK. He said: "In the next couple of years, I reckon I will be moving abroad... putting it out there." Given the "couple of years" deadline is looming at the end of 2026 - could this weekend by Keane's final World Cup working for ITV? When asked where he'd move, Keane added: "Do you know what, Switzerland, maybe? Switzerland is beautiful."

title: Harry Maguire had different first name before he legally changed it due to unwanted nickname

input.lead: Football Harry Maguire Harry Maguire had different first name before he legally changed it due to unwanted nickname Harry Maguire revealed the reasons behind his name change Comments Sport Jack Patmore 04:30, 18 Jul 2026 View 3 Images Harry Maguire opened up about his name Manche
input.summary: Football Harry Maguire Harry Maguire had different first name before he legally changed it due to unwanted nickname Harry Maguire revealed the reasons behind his name change Comments Sport Jack Patmore 04:30, 18 Jul 2026 View 3 Images Harry Maguire opened up about his name Manche
input.evidence_text: Football Harry Maguire Harry Maguire had different first name before he legally changed it due to unwanted nickname Harry Maguire revealed the reasons behind his name change Comments Sport Jack Patmore 04:30, 18 Jul 2026 View 3 Images Harry Maguire opened up about his name Manchester United centre-back Harry Maguire has shared how he legally changed his birth name due to an unwanted nickname The 33-year-old isn’t the first England international to have changed his name as a youngster, with Jordan Pickford, Joe Hart and Dele Alli all taking on a new first name or surname for personal reasons Maguire is widely known as Harry across the footballing world, but very few know that Jacob was originally on his birth certificate. However, his mum didn’t like how he was being called Jakey Ahead of their trip to Tottenham Hotspur in November, Maguire revealed to TNT Sports how the consistent use of his nickname coincided with his mum wanting to change his name. "My mum decided to call me Jacob Harry (Maguire) when I was born," he explained "Then, after three months, people started calling me Jakey. She didn’t like it so she swapped it around to Harry Jacob (Maguire) but she couldn’t change it by passport until I was 18. As soon as I was 18, I changed it around, so it’s actually officially Harry Jacob now."

But for this weekend only, shoppers can effectively get them for free if they create a TopCashback account The site is paying new members an increased £21 sign-up bonus (usually £15) when spending £15 or more, plus cashback on orders placed by 11.59pm on Sunday (July 19). When combined, the Man City shorts are effectively reduced to £0 after cashback, with £1.09 left to pay for standa This deal is for new TopCashback members only, while existing members will pay £22.09 after cashback including delivery. Both prices end up cheaper than online stores like Classic Football Shirts , where the same shorts cost £34.99 Man City’s players wore these shorts during their Premier League winning 2023/24 season. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! input.evidence_text: 8 ноября 2026 года в Indigo at The O2 состоится концерт GORAN BREGOVIC в Лондоне Имя Горана Бреговича давно стало символом целого музыкального мира. Его путь начался в многонациональном Сараеве, где восточные мотивы естественно переплетались с западными традициями, а каждая улица имела свой ритм. Именно эта атмосфера сформировала композитора, впоследствии воз Впоследствии музыка Бреговича вышла за рамки концертных площадок. Сотрудничество с режиссером Emir Kusturica подарило мировому кинематографу саундтреки, ставшие неотъемлемой частью фильмов Time of the Gypsies, Arizona Dream и Underground. В этих мелодиях живут Балканы — с их своб Популярность Бреговича родилась не только благодаря знаменитым композициям “Kalashnikov”, “Ederlezi”, “In the Death Car” или “Bella Ciao”. Его главный талант — превращать народные мотивы в современное искусство, не теряя их подлинной силы. Вместе с легендарным “Wedding and Funera Окунитесь в этот балканский водоворот прямо в самом сердце Лондона! Город, который веками принимал выступления и разные культуры со всех континентов, на один вечер станет местом встречи британской сдержанности и безудержной балканской страсти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Between them, Woodford could see more than 1,300 homes built Plans have been put forward to build the new homes off Chester Road in Woodford. The development would include up to 423 new homes as well as an extra care facility for those over 55 with up to 72 beds Alongside this, there are plans for shops, food and drink venues up to 100 square metres, new health services, a nursery or day centre, as well as a new nature park Approximately 2,900 people live in Woodford according to the latest estimates for 2024 and in the 2021 Census, 858 households were recorded In total, 567 homes could be built if the plans are given the go ahead by Stockport Council. input.evidence_text: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development A 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could cut journey times between Manchester and Sheffield by 30 minutes, according to an ambitious proposal A Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead The Woodhead railway line between the major northern cities would also be restored under the plans. The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 This scheme, named 'Trans-Pennine Connect', aims to make transport between south Yorkshire and the north west better. By putting that traffic underground, they say they can hand the Peak District landscape back to nature This in turn, they say, could boost the productivity of the region and inject millions into the local economy. But the plan goes far beyond that Trafford's Wharfside Regeneration Masterplan promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play Council leader Tom Ross wants to transform the land around the Old Trafford football grounds into a place where new and existing residents and businesses can thrive. He has lauded the Wharfside regeneration masterplan a 'foundation' for 'future success' in Trafford He said: "What other local authority wouldn't tear their right arm off to have the opportunity to be able to create something like this? title: Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena

input.lead: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0
input.summary: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0 Manchester United have confirmed the club's new 100,000-seater stadium will be built on land recently purchased from Indurent, situated roughly 350 metres from Old Trafford United confirmed the location for the new stadium on Thursday in the Wharfside Strategic Masterplan and while the stadium won't be built on land currently connected to Old Trafford, they insist it will honour the current ground's history and traditions The new ground will be the centrepiece of a Stadium District, which will be purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity, with the new stadium serving as the flagship landmark of the wider Trafford Wharfside development The masterplan sets out a bold vision for Trafford Wharfside, including new and improved public transport links, enhanced rail connectivity, and extensive walking and cycling infrastructure. The vision is for a diverse neighbourhood creating 48,000 local jobs and 15,000 new homes It is estimated it could offer a £7.3bn per year boost to the UK economy, reports the Manchester Evening News
